stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Factors influencing brood size (the number of eggs laid at one time) have been well studied in songbirds but not in salamanders.
  • A team led by Hana Park of the University of Oslo investigated which factors influence salamander brood size.
  • Park's team gathered brood-size and habitat data for more than 2,800 salamander species.
  • The team analyzed the data using statistical regression.
  • Larger brood size was associated with cooler, more seasonally variable environments.
stem: The student wants to emphasize the aim of the research study. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: After gathering data for more than 2,800 salamander species, researchers found that larger brood size was associated with cooler, more seasonally variable environments.
Researchers wanted to know which factors influence salamander brood size, because such factors have been well studied in songbirds but not in salamanders.
We now know that salamanders in cooler, more seasonally variable environments may lay larger broods to take advantage of shorter favorable windows.
Researchers gathered brood-size and habitat data for more than 2,800 salamander species and analyzed the data with statistical regression.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer because it emphasizes the aim—identifying factors that influence salamander brood size—and explains the gap that motivated the study.

Choices A, C, and D describe methods or findings rather than the study's aim.
